pattern: Separate the two independent quantities in the string - how many spaces exist total, and how many gaps between words need filling - then divide one by the other and park the remainder at the end.
hints: The letters themselves never move; only count how many space characters exist in total and split the text into its words.
With w words there are only w-1 internal gaps to fill evenly - divide the total space count by that gap count to get the even amount per gap, and whatever does not divide evenly is leftover.

Join the words with the even amount of spaces between each pair, then append the leftover spaces at the very end; when there is only a single word, all of the spaces go at the end since there are no internal gaps.

edge_inputs.description: spaces split evenly among several words with none left over
spaces do not divide evenly, leftover goes at the end
only one word in the text, all spaces go at the end
a single character, one word, no spaces at all
single word with no leading or trailing spaces at all
exactly two words separated by the minimum one space
many words but very few spaces, so most gaps get zero spaces and only the leftover shows up at the end
input: a b c d e f g h

edge_inputs.description: spaces only appear around a single word, none between words, all collapse to the end

stress.description: near-max length with a variable number of words and spaces distributed randomly
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    limit = 100
    word_count = rng.randint(1, 20)
    words = []
    for _ in range(word_count):
        wlen = rng.randint(1, 5)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(wlen)))
    total_word_len = sum(len(w) for w in words)
    min_gaps = max(word_count - 1, 0)
    remaining = limit - total_word_len
    if remaining < min_gaps:
        # trim words to fit if the random draw was too long
        words = words[:1]
        total_word_len = len(words[0])
        word_count = 1
        min_gaps = 0
        remaining = limit - total_word_len
    extra_leading = rng.randint(0, max(remaining - min_gaps, 0))
    remaining -= extra_leading
    if word_count == 1:
        text = words[0] + (' ' * remaining) if remaining > 0 else words[0]
        text = (' ' * extra_leading) + text
    else:
        gap_budget = remaining
        gaps = [1] * (word_count - 1)
        leftover = gap_budget - (word_count - 1)
        idx = 0
        while leftover > 0:
            gaps[idx % len(gaps)] += 1
            leftover -= 1
            idx += 1
        parts = [words[0]]
        for w, g in zip(words[1:], gaps):
            parts.append(' ' * g)
            parts.append(w)
        text = (' ' * extra_leading) + ''.join(parts)
    if len(text) > limit:
        text = text[:limit]
    if not text.strip():
        text = 'a'
    return [text]
